revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=高見神社| 所在地=福岡県北九州市八幡東区高見1丁目1-1| 祭神=天之御中主神
高御産巣日神
神産巣日神
可美葦芽彦遅神
天之常立神
国之常立神
豊雲野神
天照大御神
天忍穂耳神
皇孫瓊瓊杵命
穂穂手見命
穂穂手見命
鵜草葺不合命
仁徳天皇
石之比売命
多紀理毘売命
市寸島比売命
多岐都比売命
大己貴命
豊受姫命| 社格=無格社| 創建=不明| 例祭=[[10月16日]]・[[10月17日|17日]]| 神事=筑前御殿神楽| }} '''高見神社'''（たかみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[八幡東区]]にある[[神社]]。 ==祭神== 本社に天之御中主神、高御産巣日神、神産巣日神、可美葦芽彦遅神、天之常立神、国之常立神、豊雲野神、天照大御神、天忍穂耳神、皇孫瓊瓊杵命、穂穂手見命、穂穂手見命、鵜草葺不合命の十二神を祀り、相殿に仁徳天皇、石之比売命、多紀理毘売命、市寸島比売命、多岐都比売命、大己貴命、豊受姫命を祀る。 ==歴史== [[神功皇后]]の三韓征伐の際に戦勝祈願の為、洞海湾の小山に皇祖神を祀ったのが創始とされる。明治２９年に官営八幡製鐵所が建設されると、境内地が製鉄所の用地となった為、尾倉八幡神社へ遷座される。やがて日本製鐵株式会社の創立を機に製鉄の守護神として現在地に鎮座した。 社殿は完成までに１０年もの歳月を要し、総檜木流造の壮大なものである。昭和二十九年、[[昭和天皇]]の九州巡幸の際に、福岡県下の旧官幣大社とともに、とくに幣帛を賜る栄誉を得た。 {{DEFAULTSORT:たかみしんしや}} [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:八幡東区]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=高見神社| 所在地=福岡県北九州市八幡東区高見1丁目1-1| 祭神=天之御中主神
高御産巣日神
神産巣日神
可美葦芽彦遅神
天之常立神
国之常立神
豊雲野神
天照大御神
天忍穂耳神
皇孫瓊瓊杵命
穂穂手見命
穂穂手見命
鵜草葺不合命
仁徳天皇
石之比売命
多紀理毘売命
市寸島比売命
多岐都比売命
大己貴命
豊受姫命| 社格=無格社| 創建=不明| 例祭=[[10月16日]]・[[10月17日|17日]]| 神事=筑前御殿神楽| }} '''高見神社'''（たかみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[八幡東区]]にある[[神社]]。 ==祭神== 本社に天之御中主神、高御産巣日神、神産巣日神、可美葦芽彦遅神、天之常立神、国之常立神、豊雲野神、天照大御神、天忍穂耳神、皇孫瓊瓊杵命、穂穂手見命、穂穂手見命、鵜草葺不合命の十二神を祀り、相殿に仁徳天皇、石之比売命、多紀理毘売命、市寸島比売命、多岐都比売命、大己貴命、豊受姫命を祀る。 ==歴史== [[神功皇后]]の[[三韓征伐]]の際に戦勝祈願の為、[[洞海湾]]の小山に[[皇祖神]]を祀ったのが創始とされる。{{jdate|1896}}に[[八幡製鐵所|官営八幡製鐵所]]が建設されると、境内地が製鉄所の用地となった為、尾倉八幡神社へ遷座される。やがて[[日本製鐵]]の創立を機に製鉄の守護神として現在地に鎮座した。 社殿は完成までに10年もの歳月を要し、総檜木流造の壮大なものである。{{jdate|1954}}、[[昭和天皇]]の九州巡幸の際に、福岡県下の旧官幣大社とともに、とくに幣帛を賜る栄誉を得た。 {{DEFAULTSORT:たかみしんしや}} [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:八幡東区]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[豊受姫命]] |社格= 無格社 |創建= 不明 |例祭= [[10月16日]]・[[10月17日|17日]] |神事= 筑前御殿神楽 }} '''高見神社'''（たかみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[八幡東区]]にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == 本社に[[天之御中主神]]、[[高御産巣日神]]、[[神産巣日神]]、[[可美葦芽彦遅神]]、[[天之常立神]]、[[国之常立神]]、[[豊雲野神]]、[[天照大御神]]、[[天忍穂耳神]]、[[皇孫瓊瓊杵命]]、[[穂穂手見命]]、[[穂穂手見命]]、[[鵜草葺不合命]]の十二神を祀り、相殿に[[仁徳天皇]]、[[石之比売命]]、[[多紀理毘売命]]、[[市寸島比売命]]、[[多岐都比売命]]、[[大己貴命]]、[[豊受姫命]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[神功皇后]]の[[三韓征伐]]の際に戦勝祈願の為、[[洞海湾]]の小山に[[皇祖神]]を祀ったのが創始とされる。[[1896年]]（[[明治]]29年）に[[八幡製鐵所|官営八幡製鐵所]]が建設されると、境内地が製鉄所の用地となった為、尾倉八幡神社へ遷座される。やがて[[日本製鐵]]の創立を機に製鉄の守護神として現在地に鎮座した。 社殿は完成までに10年もの歳月を要し、総檜木流造の壮大なものである。[[1954年]]（[[昭和]]29年）、[[昭和天皇]]の九州巡幸の際に、福岡県下の旧官幣大社とともに、とくに幣帛を賜る栄誉を得た。 == 桜の名所 == 神社および周辺は[[桜]]の名所となっている[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 北九州市のホームページ]八幡高見地区 ～桜と水辺とふれあいの街～。 == 脚注 == ==外部リンク== *[http://www.takamijinjya.com/ 高見神社] {{DEFAULTSORT:たかみしんしや}} [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:八幡東区]] [[Category:福岡県の旧無格社]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[豊受姫命]] |社格= 無格社 |創建= 不明 |例祭= [[10月16日]]・[[10月17日|17日]] |神事= 筑前御殿神楽 }} '''高見神社'''（たかみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[八幡東区]]にある[[神社]]。 == 祭神 == 本社に[[天之御中主神]]、[[高御産巣日神]]、[[神産巣日神]]、[[可美葦芽彦遅神]]、[[天之常立神]]、[[国之常立神]]、[[豊雲野神]]、[[天照大御神]]、[[天忍穂耳神]]、[[皇孫瓊瓊杵命]]、[[穂穂手見命]]、[[穂穂手見命]]、[[鵜草葺不合命]]の十二神を祀り、相殿に[[仁徳天皇]]、[[石之比売命]]、[[多紀理毘売命]]、[[市寸島比売命]]、[[多岐都比売命]]、[[大己貴命]]、[[豊受姫命]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[神功皇后]]の[[三韓征伐]]の際に戦勝祈願の為、[[洞海湾]]の小山に[[皇祖神]]を祀ったのが創始とされる。[[1896年]]（[[明治]]29年）に[[八幡製鐵所|官営八幡製鐵所]]が建設されると、境内地が製鉄所の用地となった為、尾倉八幡神社へ遷座される。やがて[[日本製鐵]]の創立を機に製鉄の守護神として現在地に鎮座した。 社殿は完成までに10年もの歳月を要し、総檜木流造の壮大なものである。[[1954年]]（[[昭和]]29年）、[[昭和天皇]]の九州巡幸の際に、福岡県下の旧官幣大社とともに、とくに幣帛を賜る栄誉を得た。 == 桜の名所 == 神社および周辺は[[桜]]の名所となっている[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 北九州市のホームページ]八幡高見地区 ～桜と水辺とふれあいの街～。 == 脚注 == ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} *[http://www.takamijinjya.com/ 高見神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかみしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:八幡東区の歴史]] [[Category:福岡県の旧無格社]]

[[豊受姫命]] |社格= 無格社 |創建= 不明 |例祭= [[10月16日]]・[[10月17日|17日]] |神事= 筑前御殿神楽 }} '''高見神社'''（たかみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[八幡東区]]にある[[神社]]。 [[日本製鉄]][[日本製鉄九州製鉄所八幡地区|八幡製鐵所]]の守護神として鎮座している。 == 祭神 == 本社に[[天之御中主神]]、[[高御産巣日神]]、[[神産巣日神]]、[[可美葦芽彦遅神]]、[[天之常立神]]、[[国之常立神]]、[[豊雲野神]]、[[天照大御神]]、[[天忍穂耳神]]、[[皇孫瓊瓊杵命]]、[[穂穂手見命]]、[[穂穂手見命]]、[[鵜草葺不合命]]の十二神を祀り、相殿に[[仁徳天皇]]、[[石之比売命]]、[[多紀理毘売命]]、[[市寸島比売命]]、[[多岐都比売命]]、[[大己貴命]]、[[豊受姫命]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[神功皇后]]の[[三韓征伐]]の際に戦勝祈願の為、[[洞海湾]]の小山に[[皇祖神]]を祀ったのが創始とされる。[[1896年]]（[[明治]]29年）に[[官営八幡製鐵所]]が建設されると、境内地が製鉄所の用地となった為、尾倉八幡神社へ遷座される。やがて[[日本製鐵]]の創立を機に製鉄の守護神として現在地に鎮座した。 社殿は完成までに10年もの歳月を要し、総檜木流造の壮大なものである。[[1954年]]（[[昭和]]29年）、[[昭和天皇]]の九州巡幸の際に、福岡県下の旧官幣大社とともに、とくに幣帛を賜る栄誉を得た。 == 桜の名所 == 神社および周辺は[[桜]]の名所となっている[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 北九州市のホームページ]八幡高見地区 ～桜と水辺とふれあいの街～。 == 脚注 == ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} *[http://www.takamijinjya.com/ 高見神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかみしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の旧無格社]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:八幡東区の歴史]] [[category:八幡東区の建築物]] [[Category:桜に関する場所]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Takami Shrine | Image = [[File:Takami Shrine (Kitakyushu).jpg|250px]] | Address = 1-1-1 Takami, Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates= {{Coord|33|51|59.3|N|130|49|28.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}
{{ill|Umashiashikabihikoji|ja|可美葦芽彦遅神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi|ja|天之常立神}}
{{ill|Kunino-Tokotachi|ja|国之常立神}}
{{ill|Toyokumo-no-kami|ja|豊雲野神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳神}}
{{ill|Ninigi|ja|皇孫瓊瓊杵命}}
{{ill|Hikohohodemi|ja|穂穂手見命}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Ishi-no-Hime|ja|石之比売命}}
{{ill|Tagiri-hime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命}}
{{ill|Taki-tsu-hime|ja|多岐都比売命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | Founded = Unknown | Annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main Festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Tagiri-hime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dokai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]]

{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | Founded = Unknown | Annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main Festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Tagiri-hime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dokai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Check_everybodywiki}}[[Category:original pages]]

{{ill|Taki-tsu-hime|ja|多岐都比売命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Tagiri-hime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dokai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]]

{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}
{{ill|Umashiashikabihikoji|ja|可美葦芽彦遅神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi|ja|天之常立神}}
{{ill|Kunino-Tokotachi|ja|国之常立神}}
{{ill|Toyokumo-no-kami|ja|豊雲野神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳神}}
{{ill|Ninigi|ja|皇孫瓊瓊杵命}}
{{ill|Hikohohodemi|ja|穂穂手見命}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Ishi-no-Hime|ja|石之比売命}}
{{ill|Tagiri-hime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命}}
{{ill|Taki-tsu-hime|ja|多岐都比売命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Tagiri-hime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dokai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]]

{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Tagiri-hime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dokai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Pages that were the oldest for a long time so I am bumping them up the queue for this round]][[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages that were the oldest for a long time so I am bumping them up the queue for this round]][[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Pages that were the oldest for a long time so I am bumping them up the queue for this round]][[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Pages that were the oldest for a long time so I am bumping them up the queue for this round]][[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Pages that were the oldest for a long time so I am bumping them up the queue for this round]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages that were the oldest for a long time so I am bumping them up the queue for this round]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages that were the oldest for a long time so I am bumping them up the queue for this round]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:高見神社]]

It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:高見神社]]

{{ill|Umashiashikabihikoji|ja|可美葦芽彦遅神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi|ja|天之常立神}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja|国之常立神}}
{{ill|Toyokumonu|ja|豊雲野神}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳神}}
{{ill|Ninigi|ja|皇孫瓊瓊杵命}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|穂穂手見命}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Ishi-no-Hime|ja|石之比売命}}
{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}

{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|lt=Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[ja:高見神社]]

It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:高見神社]]

Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[ja:高見神社]]

It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Takami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Takami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Takami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:高見神社]]

{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳神}}

{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}

{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|lt=Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Takami Shrine]][[ja:高見神社]]

Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Takami Shrine]][[ja:高見神社]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Takami Shrine | image = [[File:Takami Shrine (Kitakyushu).jpg|250px]] | location = 1-1-1 Takami, Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates= {{Coord|33|51|59.3|N|130|49|28.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}
{{ill|Umashiashikabihikoji|ja|可美葦芽彦遅神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi|ja|天之常立神}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja|国之常立神}}
{{ill|Toyokumonu|ja|豊雲野神|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳神}}
{{ill|Ninigi|ja|皇孫瓊瓊杵命}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|穂穂手見命}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Ishi-no-Hime|ja|石之比売命}}
{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|lt=Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Takami Shrine]][[ja:高見神社]]

{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|lt=Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Takami Shrine]][[ja:高見神社]] [[Category:New]]

Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Takami Shrine]][[ja:高見神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Takami Shrine | image = [[File:Takami Shrine (Kitakyushu).jpg|250px]] | location = 1-1-1 Takami, Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates= {{Coord|33|51|59.3|N|130|49|28.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}
{{ill|Umashiashikabihikoji|ja|可美葦芽彦遅神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi|ja|天之常立神}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja|国之常立神}}
{{ill|Toyokumonu|ja|豊雲野神|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳神}}
{{ill|Ninigi|ja|皇孫瓊瓊杵命}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|穂穂手見命}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Ishi-no-Hime|ja|石之比売命}}
{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|lt=Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Takami Shrine]][[ja:高見神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Takami Shrine | image = [[File:Takami Shrine (Kitakyushu).jpg|250px]] | location = 1-1-1 Takami, Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates= {{Coord|33|51|59.3|N|130|49|28.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}

{{ill|Umashiashikabihikoji|ja|可美葦芽彦遅神}}

{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|lt=Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Takami Shrine | image = [[File:Takami Shrine (Kitakyushu).jpg|250px]] | location = 1-1-1 Takami, Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates= {{Coord|33|51|59.3|N|130|49|28.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami}}
{{ill|Umashiashikabihikoji|ja|可美葦芽彦遅神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi|ja|天之常立神}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja|国之常立神}}
{{ill|Toyokumonu|ja|豊雲野神|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳神}}
{{ill|Ninigi|ja|皇孫瓊瓊杵命}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|穂穂手見命}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}
{{ill|Ishi-no-Hime|ja|石之比売命}}
{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸島比売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}

{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|lt=Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Takami Shrine | image = [[File:Takami Shrine (Kitakyushu).jpg|250px]] | location = 1-1-1 Takami, Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates= {{Coord|33|51|59.3|N|130|49|28.9|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=draft}}
{{ill|Umashiashikabihikoji|ja|可美葦芽彦遅神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Tokotachi|ja|天之常立神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|ja|国之常立神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Toyokumonu|ja|豊雲野神|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ninigi|ja|皇孫瓊瓊杵命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|穂穂手見命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ishi-no-Hime|ja|石之比売命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸島比売命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|12=draft}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|lt=Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|12=draft}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=draft}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾|12=draft}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|12=draft}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇|12=draft}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Takami Shrine]] [[ja:高見神社]] [[en:Takami Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}}

{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} | Status = UnRanked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|lt=Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Takami Shrine]] [[ja:高見神社]] [[en:Takami Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |画像= [[File:Takami Shrine (Kitakyushu).jpg|250px]] |名称= 高見神社 |所在地= [[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[八幡東区]][[高見 (北九州市)|高見]]1丁目1-1 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|51|59.3|N|130|49|28.9|E|region:JP}} |祭神= [[天之御中主神]]
[[高御産巣日神]]
[[神産巣日神]]
[[可美葦芽彦遅神]]
[[天之常立神]]
[[国之常立神]]
[[豊雲野神]]
[[天照大御神]]
[[天忍穂耳神]]
[[瓊瓊杵|皇孫瓊瓊杵命]]
[[穂穂手見命]]
[[穂穂手見命]]
[[鵜葺草葺不合命]]
[[仁徳天皇]]
[[石之比売命]]
[[多紀理毘売命]]
[[市寸島比売命]]
[[多岐都比売命]]
[[大己貴命]]
[[豊受姫命]] |社格= 無格社 |創建= 不明 |例祭= [[10月16日]]・[[10月17日|17日]] |神事= 筑前御殿神楽 }} '''高見神社'''（たかみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[八幡東区]]にある[[神社]]。 [[日本製鉄]][[日本製鉄九州製鉄所八幡地区|八幡製鐵所]]の守護神として鎮座している。 == 祭神 == 本社に[[天之御中主神]]、[[高御産巣日神]]、[[神産巣日神]]、[[ウマシアシカビヒコヂ|可美葦芽彦遅神]]、[[天之常立神]]、[[国之常立神]]、[[豊雲野神]]、[[天照大御神]]、[[アメノオシホミミ|天忍穂耳神]]、[[瓊瓊杵|皇孫瓊瓊杵命]]、[[穂穂手見命]]、[[穂穂手見命]]、[[鵜葺草葺不合命]]の十二神を祀り、相殿に[[仁徳天皇]]、[[石之比売命]]、[[多紀理毘売命]]、[[市寸島比売命]]、[[多岐都比売命]]、[[大己貴命]]、[[豊受姫命]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[神功皇后]]の[[三韓征伐]]の際に戦勝祈願の為、[[洞海湾]]の小山に[[皇祖神]]を祀ったのが創始とされる。[[1896年]]（[[明治]]29年）に[[官営八幡製鐵所]]が建設されると、境内地が製鉄所の用地となった為、尾倉八幡神社へ遷座される。やがて[[日本製鐵]]の創立を機に製鉄の守護神として現在地に鎮座した。 社殿は完成までに10年もの歳月を要し、総檜木流造の壮大なものである。[[1954年]]（[[昭和]]29年）、[[昭和天皇]]の九州巡幸の際に、福岡県下の旧官幣大社とともに、とくに幣帛を賜る栄誉を得た。 == 桜の名所 == 神社および周辺は[[桜]]の名所となっている[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 北九州市のホームページ]八幡高見地区 ～桜と水辺とふれあいの街～。 == 脚注 == ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} *[http://www.takamijinjya.com/ 高見神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかみしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の旧無格社]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:八幡東区の歴史]] [[Category:八幡東区の建築物]] [[Category:桜に関する場所]]

{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Status = UnRanked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|lt=Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Takami Shrine]] [[ja:高見神社]] [[en:Takami Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takami Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|高見神社|version=99467334|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|高見神社|version=99467334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Takami Shrine]] [[ja:高見神社]] [[en:Takami Shrine]]

{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|lt=Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyushu|qq=}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkai Bay|qq=}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|qq=}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa Emperor|qq=}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|高見神社|version=99467334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]] }} [[sn:Takami Shrine]] [[ja:高見神社]] [[en:Takami Shrine]]

{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|lt=Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyushu|qq=}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkai Bay|qq=}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|qq=}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa Emperor|qq=}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|高見神社|version=99467334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Takami Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takami Shrine]] [[ja:高見神社]] [[en:Takami Shrine]]
