{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}
{{ill|Ishi-no-Hime|ja|石之比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi-no-Hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|lt=Yahatahigashi-ku|ja|八幡東区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyushu|qq=}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|ja|洞海湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkai Bay|qq=}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|qq=}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa Emperor|qq=}} during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|高見神社|version=99467334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Yahatahigashi-ku]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takami Shrine]] [[ja:高見神社]] [[en:Takami Shrine]]

{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|天忍穂耳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi|qq=}}
{{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|皇孫瓊瓊杵命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}}
{{ill|Hoori|ja|穂穂手見命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}
{{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu|qq=}}
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
{{ill|Ishi-no-Hime|ja|石之比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi-no-Hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatahigashi-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking [[:en:Dōkai Bay|Dōkai Bay]], it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|qq=}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the [[:en:Hirohito|Shōwa Emperor]] during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|高見神社|version=99467334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takami Shrine]] [[ja:高見神社]] [[en:Takami Shrine]]

{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatahigashi-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking [[:en:Dōkai Bay|Dōkai Bay]], it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|qq=}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the [[:en:Hirohito|Shōwa Emperor]] during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|高見神社|version=99467334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takami Shrine]] [[ja:高見神社]] [[en:Takami Shrine]] [[Category:八幡東区の建築物]] [[Category:八幡東区の歴史]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]]

It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking [[:en:Dōkai Bay|Dōkai Bay]], it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|qq=}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the [[:en:Hirohito|Shōwa Emperor]] during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|高見神社|version=99467334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takami Shrine]] [[ja:高見神社]] [[Category:八幡東区の建築物]] [[Category:八幡東区の歴史]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:Hanami spots of Japan]] [[Category:福岡県の旧無格社]]

It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|qq=}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking [[:en:Dōkai Bay|Dōkai Bay]], it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|qq=}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the [[:en:Hirohito|Shōwa Emperor]] during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|高見神社|version=99467334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takami Shrine]] [[ja:高見神社]] [[Category:八幡東区の建築物]] [[Category:八幡東区の歴史]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hanami spots of Japan]] [[Category:福岡県の旧無格社]] {{Commons category|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}}

{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatahigashi-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本製鉄九州製鉄所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking [[:en:Dōkai Bay|Dōkai Bay]], it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡製鉄所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the [[:en:Hirohito|Shōwa Emperor]] during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|高見神社|version=99467334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Takami Shrine]] [[ja:高見神社]] [[Category:八幡東区の建築物]] [[Category:八幡東区の歴史]] [[Category:Shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hanami spots of Japan]] [[Category:福岡県の旧無格社]] {{Commons category|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}}

[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
{{ill|Ishi-no-Hime|ja|石之比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi-no-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸島比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatahigashi-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本製鉄九州製鉄所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking [[:en:Dōkai Bay|Dōkai Bay]], it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡製鉄所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the [[:en:Hirohito|Shōwa Emperor]] during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|高見神社|version=99467334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Takami Shrine]] }} [[sn:Takami Shrine]] [[ja:高見神社]] [[Category:八幡東区の建築物]] [[Category:八幡東区の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hanami spots of Japan]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}}

In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the [[:en:Hirohito|Shōwa Emperor]] during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|高見神社|version=99467334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Takami Shrine]] }} [[sn:Takami Shrine]] [[ja:高見神社]] [[Category:八幡東区の建築物]] [[Category:八幡東区の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hanami spots of Japan]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}}

{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Toyouke-hime|ja|豊受姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) }} '''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatahigashi-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本製鉄九州製鉄所}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking [[:en:Dōkai Bay|Dōkai Bay]], it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡製鉄所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking. The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the [[:en:Hirohito|Shōwa Emperor]] during his tour of Kyushu. == Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website. == Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area. == References == == External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|高見神社|version=99467334|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Takami Shrine]] }} [[sn:Takami Shrine]] [[ja:高見神社]] [[Category:八幡東区の建築物]] [[Category:八幡東区の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Hanami spots of Japan]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}}

The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name       = Takami Shrine
| image      = File:Takami Shrine (Kitakyushu).jpg
| location    = 1-1-1 Takami, Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan
| coordinates= {{Coord|33.866472|N|130.824694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
| deity    = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779}}
| Status     = Unranked Shrine (無格社)
| founded    = Unknown
| annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }}
| Rituals    = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽)
| prefecture= {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku|qid=Q857895}}
}}

'''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatahigashi-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本製鉄九州製鉄所}}.

== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}.

== History ==
The shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking [[:en:Dōkai Bay|Dōkai Bay]], it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡製鉄所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking.

The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the [[:en:Hirohito|Shōwa Emperor]] during his tour of Kyushu.

== Cherry Blossom Spot ==
The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.<ref>[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website.</ref>

== Access ==
* By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area.

== References ==
<references />

== External Links ==
* {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}}

'''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatahigashi-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本製鉄九州製鉄所}}.

== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}.

== History ==
The shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking [[:en:Dōkai Bay|Dōkai Bay]], it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡製鉄所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking.

The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the [[:en:Hirohito|Shōwa Emperor]] during his tour of Kyushu.

== Cherry Blossom Spot ==
The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.<ref>[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website.</ref>

== Access ==
* By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area.

| prefecture= {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku|WD=Q857895}}
}}

'''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatahigashi-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本製鉄九州製鉄所}}.

== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}.

== History ==
The shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking [[:en:Dōkai Bay|Dōkai Bay]], it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡製鉄所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking.

The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the [[:en:Hirohito|Shōwa Emperor]] during his tour of Kyushu.

== Cherry Blossom Spot ==
The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.<ref>[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website.</ref>

== Access ==
* By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area.

== References ==
<references />

== External Links ==
* {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}}

'''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatahigashi-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本製鉄九州製鉄所}}.

== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}.

== History ==
The shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking [[:en:Dōkai Bay|Dōkai Bay]], it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡製鉄所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking.

The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the [[:en:Hirohito|Shōwa Emperor]] during his tour of Kyushu.

== Cherry Blossom Spot ==
The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.<ref>[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website.</ref>

== Access ==
* By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area.

The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}.

== History ==
The shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking [[:en:Dōkai Bay|Dōkai Bay]], it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡製鉄所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking.

The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the [[:en:Hirohito|Shōwa Emperor]] during his tour of Kyushu.

== Cherry Blossom Spot ==
The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.<ref>[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website.</ref>

== Access ==
* By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "History of Yahatahigashi Ward" to "History of Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyushu"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)]][[ja:高見神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)]][[ja:高見神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)]][[ja:高見神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11672831]] Wikidata name: Takami-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Takami Shrine | image = File:Takami Shrine (Kitakyushu).jpg | location = 1-1-1 Takami, Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates= {{Coord|33.866472|N|130.824694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) | prefecture= {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku|WD=Q857895}} }}
'''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatahigashi-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本製鉄九州製鉄所}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]], {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}.
== History == The shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking [[:en:Dōkai Bay|Dōkai Bay]], it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡製鉄所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking.
The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the [[:en:Hirohito|Shōwa Emperor]] during his tour of Kyushu.
== Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.<ref>[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website.</ref>
== Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area.
== References == <references />
== External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}}
{{Shinto-stub}}

'''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|Yahatahigashi-ku]], [[:en:Kitakyushu|Kitakyushu]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本製鉄九州製鉄所}}.

Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}.
== History == The shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking [[:en:Dōkai Bay|Dōkai Bay]], it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡製鉄所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking.
The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the [[:en:Hirohito|Shōwa Emperor]] during his tour of Kyushu.
== Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.<ref>[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website.</ref>
== Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area.
== References == <references />

Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|en|Dōkai Bay|WD=Q11555848|lt=Dōkai Bay}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡製鉄所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking.
The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Shōwa Emperor}} during his tour of Kyushu.
== Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.<ref>[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website.</ref>
== Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area.

It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本製鉄九州製鉄所}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}.

Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|en|Dōkai Bay|WD=Q11555848|lt=Dōkai Bay}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡製鉄所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking.
The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Shōwa Emperor}} during his tour of Kyushu.
== Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.<ref>[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Takami Shrine | image = File:Takami Shrine (Kitakyushu).jpg | location = 1-1-1 Takami, Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyushu, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates= {{Coord|33.866472|N|130.824694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} | Status = Unranked Shrine (無格社) | founded = Unknown | annual Events = {{bulleted list | October 16–17: Main festival }} | Rituals = Chikuzen Goten Kagura (筑前御殿神楽) | prefecture= {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku|WD=Q857895|1=Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū}} }}
'''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|en|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|WD=Q857895|lt=Yahatahigashi-ku|1=Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|en|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806|lt=Kitakyushu|1=Kitakyushu}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本製鉄九州製鉄所}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}.
== History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|en|Dōkai Bay|WD=Q11555848|lt=Dōkai Bay|1=Dōkai Bay}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡製鉄所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking.
The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Shōwa Emperor|1=Hirohito}} during his tour of Kyushu.
== Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.<ref>[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website.</ref>
== Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area.
== References == <references />
== External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}}

'''Takami Shrine''' (たかみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|en|Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū|WD=Q857895|lt=Yahatahigashi-ku|1=Yahatahigashi-ku, Kitakyūshū}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|en|Kitakyushu|WD=Q188806|lt=Kitakyushu|1=Kitakyushu}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It serves as the guardian shrine for the {{ill|Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja|日本製鉄八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippon Steel Yawata Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本製鉄九州製鉄所}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines twelve principal deities, including {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, and {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, along with other deities associated with Japanese mythology. Additionally, the auxiliary enshrined deities include {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, {{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}.
== History == The shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}’s conquest of the Korean Peninsula to pray for victory. Originally located on a hill overlooking {{ill|Dōkai Bay|en|Dōkai Bay|WD=Q11555848|lt=Dōkai Bay|1=Dōkai Bay}}, it was moved to accommodate the construction of the {{ill|Yawata Steel Works|ja|八幡製鐵所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yawata Steel Works|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡製鉄所}} in 1896. The shrine was later relocated to its current site and rebuilt as a place to honor the gods of ironworking.
The construction of the current shrine buildings, which are grand and made of Japanese cypress in the nagare-zukuri style, took ten years. In 1954, the shrine received an imperial offering from the {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Shōwa Emperor|1=Hirohito}} during his tour of Kyushu.
== Cherry Blossom Spot == The shrine and its surrounding area are renowned for cherry blossoms, attracting visitors during the spring season.<ref>[http://www.city.kitakyushu.jp/pcp_portal/PortalServlet?DISPLAY_ID=DIRECT&NEXT_DISPLAY_ID=U000004&CONTENTS_ID=3667 Takami District - Cherry Blossoms and Waterfront Attractions]. Kitakyushu City Official Website.</ref>
== Access == * By public transportation: The shrine is a short distance from local bus stops in the area.
== References == <references />
== External Links == * {{Official website|http://www.takamijinjya.com|Takami Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Takami-jinja (Kitakyushu)}}
